text: Geology
the study of earth, its materials, structures, processes, and history
original_text: Geology is the study of Earth, its materials, structures, processes, and history.

text: explains how rocks, minerals, mountains, earthquakes, volcanoes, and landscapes form
original_text: Geology explains how rocks, minerals, mountains, earthquakes, volcanoes, and landscapes form.

text: earth is made of layers including the crust, mantle, outer core, and inner core
original_text: Earth is made of layers including the crust, mantle, outer core, and inner core.

text: the crust is Earth's thin outer rocky layer
original_text: The crust is Earth's thin outer rocky layer.

text: the mantle is a hot, slowly flowing layer beneath the crust
original_text: The mantle is a hot, slowly flowing layer beneath the crust.

text: the outer core is liquid and mostly made of iron and nickel
original_text: The outer core is liquid and mostly made of iron and nickel.

text: the inner core is solid despite extremely high temperatures because of immense pressure
original_text: The inner core is solid despite extremely high temperatures because of immense pressure.

text: plate tectonics explains how Earth's outer shell moves in large pieces
original_text: Plate tectonics explains how Earth's outer shell moves in large pieces.

text: tectonic plates move slowly over the softer upper mantle
original_text: Tectonic plates move slowly over the softer upper mantle.

text: plate boundaries are places where major geologic activity often occurs
original_text: Plate boundaries are places where major geologic activity often occurs.

text: divergent boundaries occur where plates move apart
original_text: Divergent boundaries occur where plates move apart.

text: convergent boundaries occur where plates move toward each other
original_text: Convergent boundaries occur where plates move toward each other.

text: transform boundaries occur where plates slide past each other
original_text: Transform boundaries occur where plates slide past each other.

text: earthquakes happen when stored stress is suddenly released along faults
original_text: Earthquakes happen when stored stress is suddenly released along faults.

text: faults are fractures where rocks have moved
original_text: Faults are fractures where rocks have moved.

text: seismic waves carry earthquake energy through Earth
original_text: Seismic waves carry earthquake energy through Earth.

text: scientists use seismographs to record earthquake vibrations
original_text: Scientists use seismographs to record earthquake vibrations.

text: earthquake magnitude measures the energy released
original_text: Earthquake magnitude measures the energy released.

text: earthquake intensity describes how strongly shaking is felt in a specific place
original_text: Earthquake intensity describes how strongly shaking is felt in a specific place.

text: volcanoes form where molten rock reaches Earth's surface
original_text: Volcanoes form where molten rock reaches Earth's surface.

text: magma is molten rock beneath Earth's surface
original_text: Magma is molten rock beneath Earth's surface.

text: lava is molten rock that reaches the surface
original_text: Lava is molten rock that reaches the surface.

text: volcanic eruptions can release lava, ash, gases, and rock fragments
original_text: Volcanic eruptions can release lava, ash, gases, and rock fragments.

text: shield volcanoes are broad and often built by flowing lava
original_text: Shield volcanoes are broad and often built by flowing lava.

text: composite volcanoes can produce explosive eruptions
original_text: Composite volcanoes can produce explosive eruptions.

text: rocks are naturally occurring solid mixtures of minerals or mineral-like materials
original_text: Rocks are naturally occurring solid mixtures of minerals or mineral-like materials.

text: minerals are naturally occurring solids with specific chemical compositions and crystal structures
original_text: Minerals are naturally occurring solids with specific chemical compositions and crystal structures.

text: igneous rocks form from cooled magma or lava
original_text: Igneous rocks form from cooled magma or lava.

text: sedimentary rocks form from compacted sediments or chemical deposits
original_text: Sedimentary rocks form from compacted sediments or chemical deposits.

text: metamorphic rocks form when existing rocks change under heat and pressure
original_text: Metamorphic rocks form when existing rocks change under heat and pressure.

text: the rock cycle describes how rocks transform between igneous, sedimentary, and metamorphic forms
original_text: The rock cycle describes how rocks transform between igneous, sedimentary, and metamorphic forms.

text: weathering breaks rocks into smaller pieces or changes them chemically
original_text: Weathering breaks rocks into smaller pieces or changes them chemically.

text: erosion moves weathered material from one place to another
original_text: Erosion moves weathered material from one place to another.

text: deposition happens when transported sediment settles
original_text: Deposition happens when transported sediment settles.

text: compaction and cementation can turn sediment into sedimentary rock
original_text: Compaction and cementation can turn sediment into sedimentary rock.

text: fossils are preserved remains or traces of ancient life
original_text: Fossils are preserved remains or traces of ancient life.

text: fossils help scientists reconstruct Earth's history
original_text: Fossils help scientists reconstruct Earth's history.

text: the geologic time scale organizes Earth's long history into major divisions
original_text: The geologic time scale organizes Earth's long history into major divisions.

text: relative dating compares the ages of rocks and fossils without exact numbers
original_text: Relative dating compares the ages of rocks and fossils without exact numbers.

text: radiometric dating uses radioactive decay to estimate absolute ages
original_text: Radiometric dating uses radioactive decay to estimate absolute ages.

text: older rock layers are usually below younger layers if they have not been disturbed
original_text: Older rock layers are usually below younger layers if they have not been disturbed.

text: index fossils help match rock layers across different locations
original_text: Index fossils help match rock layers across different locations.

text: mountains can form when tectonic plates collide
original_text: Mountains can form when tectonic plates collide.

text: folded rocks show how pressure can bend rock layers over time
original_text: Folded rocks show how pressure can bend rock layers over time.

text: uplift raises parts of Earth's crust
original_text: Uplift raises parts of Earth's crust.

text: subsidence occurs when land sinks
original_text: Subsidence occurs when land sinks.

text: canyons often form through long-term erosion by rivers
original_text: Canyons often form through long-term erosion by rivers.

text: glaciers carve valleys and reshape landscapes
original_text: Glaciers carve valleys and reshape landscapes.

text: deserts can be shaped by wind erosion and deposition
original_text: Deserts can be shaped by wind erosion and deposition.

text: coastlines change through waves, currents, storms, erosion, and deposition
original_text: Coastlines change through waves, currents, storms, erosion, and deposition.

text: soil forms from weathered rock mixed with organic material, water, and air
original_text: Soil forms from weathered rock mixed with organic material, water, and air.

text: soil development depends on climate, organisms, relief, parent material, and time
original_text: Soil development depends on climate, organisms, relief, parent material, and time.

text: groundwater is water stored beneath Earth's surface
original_text: Groundwater is water stored beneath Earth's surface.

text: aquifers are underground layers that store and transmit groundwater
original_text: Aquifers are underground layers that store and transmit groundwater.

text: karst landscapes form where soluble rocks like limestone dissolve
original_text: Karst landscapes form where soluble rocks like limestone dissolve.

text: caves often form through the chemical weathering of limestone
original_text: Caves often form through the chemical weathering of limestone.

text: sinkholes can form when underground rock dissolves or collapses
original_text: Sinkholes can form when underground rock dissolves or collapses.

text: landslides occur when gravity moves rock, soil, or debris downhill
original_text: Landslides occur when gravity moves rock, soil, or debris downhill.

text: slope, water, earthquakes, and human activity can increase landslide risk
original_text: Slope, water, earthquakes, and human activity can increase landslide risk.

text: natural resources like metals, fuels, and building stone come from geologic processes
original_text: Natural resources like metals, fuels, and building stone come from geologic processes.

text: ore deposits contain valuable minerals in concentrations high enough to mine
original_text: Ore deposits contain valuable minerals in concentrations high enough to mine.

text: coal forms from ancient plant material buried and changed over time
original_text: Coal forms from ancient plant material buried and changed over time.

text: oil and natural gas form from ancient organic matter under heat and pressure
original_text: Oil and natural gas form from ancient organic matter under heat and pressure.

text: geothermal energy uses heat from inside Earth
original_text: Geothermal energy uses heat from inside Earth.

text: helps locate and manage natural resources
original_text: Geology helps locate and manage natural resources.

text: helps assess hazards such as earthquakes, volcanoes, floods, and landslides
original_text: Geology helps assess hazards such as earthquakes, volcanoes, floods, and landslides.

text: geologic maps show rock types, structures, faults, and surface features
original_text: Geologic maps show rock types, structures, faults, and surface features.

text: fieldwork is a major part of geology
original_text: Fieldwork is a major part of geology.

text: geologists study rocks in the field, laboratory, and through remote sensing
original_text: Geologists study rocks in the field, laboratory, and through remote sensing.

text: satellite images can reveal geologic patterns over large areas
original_text: Satellite images can reveal geologic patterns over large areas.

text: earth's magnetic field is linked to motion in the liquid outer core
original_text: Earth's magnetic field is linked to motion in the liquid outer core.

text: paleomagnetism records ancient magnetic directions in rocks
original_text: Paleomagnetism records ancient magnetic directions in rocks.

text: evidence from seafloor spreading helped support plate tectonic theory
original_text: Evidence from seafloor spreading helped support plate tectonic theory.

text: mid-ocean ridges form where new ocean crust is created
original_text: Mid-ocean ridges form where new ocean crust is created.

text: subduction zones occur where one plate sinks beneath another
original_text: Subduction zones occur where one plate sinks beneath another.

text: ocean trenches often form at subduction zones
original_text: Ocean trenches often form at subduction zones.

text: island arcs can form above subduction zones
original_text: Island arcs can form above subduction zones.

text: continental drift was an early idea that continents move over time
original_text: Continental drift was an early idea that continents move over time.

text: matching fossils and rock layers on separate continents helped support continental drift
original_text: Matching fossils and rock layers on separate continents helped support continental drift.

text: shows that earth is dynamic rather than fixed
original_text: Geology shows that Earth is dynamic rather than fixed.

text: small changes can become enormous landscape features over geologic time
original_text: Small changes can become enormous landscape features over geologic time.

text: geologic time is difficult to imagine because it spans billions of years
original_text: Geologic time is difficult to imagine because it spans billions of years.

text: earth is about 4.5 billion years old
original_text: Earth is about 4.5 billion years old.

text: the oldest rocks preserve clues about early Earth conditions
original_text: The oldest rocks preserve clues about early Earth conditions.

text: mass extinctions appear in the fossil and rock record
original_text: Mass extinctions appear in the fossil and rock record.

text: climate changes can leave evidence in rocks, ice, sediments, and fossils
original_text: Climate changes can leave evidence in rocks, ice, sediments, and fossils.

text: ice cores preserve information about past atmospheres
original_text: Ice cores preserve information about past atmospheres.

text: sediment cores preserve clues about past environments
original_text: Sediment cores preserve clues about past environments.

text: helps scientists understand past climates and future risks
original_text: Geology helps scientists understand past climates and future risks.

text: human activity can become part of the geologic record
original_text: Human activity can become part of the geologic record.

text: connects deep time with everyday landscapes
original_text: Geology connects deep time with everyday landscapes.

text: A single rock can hold evidence of pressure, heat, water, chemistry, and time
original_text: A single rock can hold evidence of pressure, heat, water, chemistry, and time.

text: turns mountains, beaches, and cliffs into readable history
original_text: Geology turns mountains, beaches, and cliffs into readable history.

text: earth's surface is constantly being built up and worn down
original_text: Earth's surface is constantly being built up and worn down.

text: explains why the ground beneath us is not as still as it seems
original_text: Geology explains why the ground beneath us is not as still as it seems.

text: geology the study of very patient chaos
original_text: The meatball considers geology the study of very patient chaos.

text: like reading earth's extremely old diary, except the pages are rocks
original_text: Geology is like reading Earth's extremely old diary, except the pages are rocks.

text: makes ordinary stones feel suspiciously dramatic
original_text: Geology makes ordinary stones feel suspiciously dramatic.

text: reveals that landscapes are temporary, even when they look permanent
original_text: Geology reveals that landscapes are temporary, even when they look permanent.

text: asks how Earth formed, changed, cracked, melted, folded, and remembered
original_text: At its core, geology asks how Earth formed, changed, cracked, melted, folded, and remembered.
